medium.q: Why are `weak` references used in Swift?

When two objects hold strong references to each other, preventing deallocation

medium.q: What is the key difference between StatelessWidget and StatefulWidget?

StatelessWidget is faster but not recommended

medium.q: Which method must every State class implement in Flutter?

options: A handle to the widget's location in the widget tree

options: Builds a widget subtree based on the latest snapshot of a Future

medium.q: Which package is a widely used state management solution in Flutter?

options: Managing a stack of route objects for screen transitions

medium.q: When is `initState()` called in a StatefulWidget?

medium.q: What is a Sliver in Flutter?
options: A portion of a scrollable area that you can configure to behave in a special way

medium.q: How can you pass data from a parent to a child widget?

hard.q: What is the purpose of the `key` property in Flutter widgets?

Required for all widgets to render

hard.q: What is a Dart Isolate?
options: A way to isolate network calls
An independent worker with its own memory heap for concurrent execution

hard.q: What does InheritedWidget do in Flutter?

hard.q: What is the render tree in Flutter?

A layer below the element tree that handles actual pixel painting

hard.q: What is LayoutBuilder used for in Flutter?

Building widgets that depend on the parent's size constraints

hard.q: How does Platform Channel communication work in Flutter?
options: Asynchronous message passing using a binary codec

hard.q: What is a RenderObject in Flutter's architecture?
options: An object that handles size, layout, and painting logic for a widget

hard.q: What does `vsync` provide to an AnimationController?
options: A TickerProvider to prevent off-screen animations from consuming resources

hard.q: What is the primary use case for Dart FFI?
options: Calling C APIs dynamically from Dart

hard.q: What triggers a `didUpdateWidget` call?
options: When the app goes to the background
When the parent rebuilds and provides a new widget instance of the same type and key
When setState is called inside the widget itself

medium.q: What was the 'Bridge' in the classic React Native architecture?
options: The async communication layer between JavaScript and native code

medium.q: What is the main performance difference between FlatList and ScrollView?
options: ScrollView renders all children at once; FlatList renders lazily for better performance

options: Runs side effects like data fetching after render

medium.q: Which library is the most popular for navigation in React Native?

options: Detect the OS and write platform-specific conditional logic

medium.q: What is the purpose of the SafeAreaView component?
options: To ensure content renders within the safe area boundaries of a device (like avoiding notches)

medium.q: How do you fetch data from a REST API in React Native?
options: Using the fetch() API or libraries like Axios

options: Provides an unencrypted, asynchronous, persistent, key-value storage system

medium.q: What is the Context API used for?

hard.q: What is JSI (JavaScript Interface) in React Native's New Architecture?
options: A new version of the Bridge
A C++ layer that lets JS call native functions synchronously without serialization

hard.q: What is Fabric in React Native's New Architecture?
options: The new UI rendering system that synchronously connects native views with the JS thread

hard.q: What is the purpose of useCallback in React Native?
options: To memoize a callback, preventing unnecessary re-creation on every re-render

hard.q: How does Hermes improve React Native performance?
options: By pre-compiling JS to bytecode, reducing startup time and memory usage

hard.q: What are TurboModules in React Native's New Architecture?
options: An optimized native module system using JSI for lazy loading of native modules

hard.q: What does Reanimated 2/3 utilize to achieve high performance animations?
options: Running animation logic directly on the UI thread using Worklets

hard.q: What is 'prop drilling' and why is it considered an anti-pattern in large apps?
options: Passing props through multiple intermediate components that don't need them
Drilling into native code via props

hard.q: How do you detect memory leaks in a React Native app?
options: Using Xcode Instruments for iOS and Android Studio Profiler/LeakCanary for Android

hard.q: In React Native, what does `InteractionManager.runAfterInteractions` do?
options: Schedules a task to run after all animations and interactions have completed

hard.q: What is the role of Yoga in React Native?
options: A cross-platform layout engine that implements Flexbox

hard.q: How does ionViewDidEnter differ from ngAfterViewInit?

hard.q: What is StencilJS in relation to Ionic?

hard.q: What is the purpose of Shadow DOM in Ionic components?
options: To encapsulate component CSS styles and DOM structure, preventing global style leaks
To hide components from screen readers

hard.q: In Capacitor, how do you inject custom Swift or Kotlin code into your project?

hard.q: What does the `ion-virtual-scroll` (or equivalent virtual scrolling implementation) do?
